LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL SANCTION; (ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR ISSUES AND EVIDENCE SANCTIONS). BOTH SIDES SUBMIT TO THE TENTATIVE RULING AND AGREE ON A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AS MODIFIED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO DISMISS THE ACTION DENIED. HOWEVER, THE COURT ORDERS THE FOLLOWING SANCTIONS FOR PLAINTIFF'S WILLFUL DESTRUCTION OF CRITICAL EVIDENCE: AT TRIAL THE COURT WILL GIVE THE WILLFUL SUPPRESSION OF EVIDENCE INSTRUCTION (CAL. CIVIL JURY INST. NO. 204) TO THE JURY, ALONG WITH PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY REGARDING HIS WILLFUL DESTRUCTION OF COMPUTER FILES AND HIS REASON FOR DOING SO. SAID DEPOSITION TESTIMONY WILL ALSO INCLUDE PORTIONS RELATED TO THE PLAINTIFF'S KNOWLEDGE THAT THE COMPUTERS WERE GOING TO BE INSPECTED BY DEFENDANT'S FORENSIC EXPERTS. ANY DISPUTES REGARDING WHAT PORTIONS OF THE DEPOSITION TO BE READ SHALL BE RESOLVED BY THE TRIAL COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Mtn For Dismissal Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC., dba ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL SANCTION; (ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR ISSUES AND EVIDENCE SANCTIONS).

APPEARANCES: Marjorie Heinrich, Esq. of Dolan Law Firm 510/893-2700 for pltf & opposing party; Michael J. Vartain, Esq. of Vartain Law Group 391-1155 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Both sides submit to the tentative ruling and agree on a proposed form of order as modified. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion to dismiss the action denied. However, the court orders the following sanctions for plaintiff's willful destruction of critical evidence: At trial the court will give the Willful Suppression of Evidence Instruction (Cal. Civil Jury Inst. No. 204) to the jury, along with plaintiff's deposition testimony regarding his willful destruction of computer files and his reason for doing so. Said deposition testimony will also include portions related to the plaintiff's knowledge that the computers were going to be inspected by defendant's forensic experts. Any disputes regarding what portions of the deposition to be read shall be resolved by the trial court. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M.
